---
description: Review unreviewed task commits and produce a self-contained review artifact under docs/agents/reviews/.
agent: plan
---

<!-- Generated by MonkeyBars. Edit workflow-src/ instead. -->

## When to use

Use after one or more tasks have been completed and committed, before the next
planning or implementation session builds on top of them. Default invocation
`/review-work` auto-scopes to the commits that have been made since the last
review. Use explicit scope arguments to re-review a specific range.

Do not use when the worktree is dirty — finish or stash the in-flight work
first. Do not use as a substitute for inline review during implementation;
this skill reviews committed work, not uncommitted diffs.

## Inputs

- Optional scope argument. One of:
  - `phase-N` — every task commit belonging to phase N.
  - `T0X..T0Y` — range of task ids, inclusive, within the active phase.
  - `<sha>..<sha>` — explicit git range.
  - `last` — most recent task commit only.
  - (no argument) — auto-scope from the last review's `reviewed_through:` to
    `HEAD`.

## Steps

1. Refuse and stop if `git status --porcelain` is non-empty. Report the dirty
   files and tell the user to commit or stash before running `/review-work`.
   Do not create a review artifact for a dirty worktree.

2. Read `docs/agents/status.md` and the active phase file it points at. These
   are reference material for Pass 1 (spec compliance) — do not modify them.

3. Resolve scope:
   - If an argument was passed, resolve it:
     - `phase-N` — find the phase file `docs/agents/work/phase-N.md`, read its
       task ids, then match commits in `git log` whose subject is
       `type(TXX): ...` for those task ids.
     - `T0X..T0Y` — same, restricted to the id range within the active phase.
     - `<sha>..<sha>` — use the range verbatim.
     - `last` — `git log -1 --format=%H`.
   - Otherwise auto-scope:
     - List `docs/agents/reviews/*.md` and pick the one with the newest
       filename (reviews are named `YYYY-MM-DD-<sha>.md`, so lexical order
       matches chronological order; fall back to file mtime on a tie).
     - Extract the `**reviewed_through:** <sha>` anchor from its body.
     - Run `git log <sha>..HEAD --oneline`. If the output is empty, stop and
       report "No unreviewed commits since `<sha>`." Do not create an empty
       review artifact.
     - Filter the log to task-shape commits (subject matches
       `^[a-z]+(\(T\d+\))?: ` with a `(TXX)` scope — e.g. `feat(T03): ...`,
       `fix(T12): ...`). Merge commits (two parents) are skipped.
     - If no prior review file exists, fall back to the task commits on the
       active phase. If the active phase has no task commits yet, fall back
       to the merge-base with `main` (or `master`) using
       `git merge-base HEAD main`.
   - Record the resolved base sha, head sha, and the commit list for use in
     the artifact. Report the scope to the user before continuing.

4. Pass 1 — spec compliance. For each commit in scope, check:
   - Does the implementation fully satisfy the task's acceptance notes from
     the phase file?
   - Are any acceptance criteria unmet or only partially met?
   - Is there scope creep, over-engineering, or added behavior not in the
     task?
   - Are there deviations from plan constraints or conventions?
   - Are there user-visible behavior changes that were not called out in the
     task?

5. Pass 2 — correctness and production risk. Check the cumulative diff
   `git diff <base>..<head>` for:
   - Correctness and likely regressions.
   - Edge cases, null/empty/boundary conditions.
   - Error handling and recovery paths.
   - Security / privacy / auth / input validation concerns.
   - State management, concurrency, idempotency, migrations, compatibility.
   - Cohesion, separation of concerns, interface clarity.
   - Test quality: do tests prove behavior, including unhappy paths?
   - Documentation, observability, rollout, backward-compatibility gaps.

6. Operating rules for both passes:
   - Do not trust the commit message. Verify by reading the diff and the
     affected code.
   - Report only concrete, patch-relevant, actionable findings. Prefer no
     finding over speculative finding.
   - Ignore pure style nits unless they harm clarity, correctness,
     maintainability, or documented standards.
   - For every finding, classify the root cause as one of:
     - spec ambiguity or missing requirement
     - implementation defect
     - pre-existing unrelated issue surfaced by the change
   - Keep evidence tight: `file:line`, triggering scenario, and impact.
   - Do not generate a patch. Brief remediation direction is enough.
   - If requirements are ambiguous, do not guess — mark the review
     `blocked by spec ambiguity`.

7. Write the artifact to `docs/agents/reviews/YYYY-MM-DD-<short-sha>.md` where
   `YYYY-MM-DD` is today's date and `<short-sha>` is the first seven chars of
   the head sha. If a file at that exact path already exists (same date, same
   head — a re-run of the same review), overwrite it. Do not create
   date-suffixed variants.

   The artifact body is:

   ```markdown
   # Review — YYYY-MM-DD

   **Scope:** <human description of the range, e.g. "phase 2, T06..T08" or
   "auto: 3 commits since 2026-04-22">
   **Base:** <base sha>
   **Head:** <head sha>
   **reviewed_through:** <head sha>
   **Verdict:** <approve | approve with non-blocking comments | changes required | blocked by spec ambiguity>

   ## Commits reviewed

   - <sha> <subject>
   - ...

   ## Summary

   2–4 sentences on overall status.

   ## Findings

   ### Blockers

   - [Category] file:line — issue
     - Why it matters:
     - Evidence:
     - Root cause: spec ambiguity | implementation defect | pre-existing unrelated issue
     - Suggested direction:

   ### Important

   (same format; omit the section entirely if none)

   ### Minor

   (same format; omit the section entirely if none)

   ## Acceptance trace

   - <task id or AC> — met | partially met | unmet — evidence

   ## Risk notes

   - Security:
   - Data / migrations:
   - Performance:
   - Operational / rollout:

   ## Next action

   Choose one: return to spec refinement | patch and re-review | add tests
   and re-review | ready to merge.
   ```

   The `**reviewed_through:**` anchor must sit on its own line and contain
   the full head sha so the next run can parse it deterministically.

8. Report the artifact path and verdict. Do not update `docs/agents/status.md`,
   `docs/agents/plan.md`, or any phase file. Do not run `git add`, `git
   commit`, or any workflow-advancing CLI.
